ranked.text: I'm Beckett, 17, building Aurelius (runaurelius.com) — constraint-aware orchestration for AI infrastructure. The thesis: AI infrastructure has a planning problem disguised as a power problem, where schedulers optimize the next placement but nothing chooses the fleet's best trajectory over time. In simulated replay of ~1.5M requests from public production traces, a predictive world model that forecasts power constraints and ranks candidate decisions by economic outcome achieved -84% GPU-hours versus the production scheduler. May I send you the one-page technical memo? May I send you the one-page technical memo? — Beckett

persona.label: model_based_judgment (LLM persona role-play; UNCALIBRATED — not a measured response probability)

sample_replies.reply: Send the memo. Why -84%? That number sounds too round. Give me the variance and the trace source.

Beckett — the thesis that the planning problem is upstream of the power problem is genuinely contrarian and specific. The -84% GPU-hours result is the kind of concrete signal that makes me read further. Send the memo to my assistant at [assistant@foundersfund.com] with subject line 'Aurelius — constraint-aware orchestration memo.' I'll look at it this week. — PT

Beckett, the thesis is genuinely contrarian. Power is the consensus bottleneck; planning is the real one. The -84% GPU-hours in replay is interesting but I'd need to understand the simulation's fidelity — did you model thermal constraints, carbon-aware scheduling, and spot-market pricing? Send the memo. — PT

ranked.text: I'm Beckett, 17, building Aurelius (runaurelius.com) — constraint-aware orchestration for AI infrastructure. The thesis: AI infrastructure has a planning problem disguised as a power problem, where schedulers optimize the next placement but nothing chooses the fleet's best trajectory over time. In simulated replay of ~1.5M public production requests, our model beat the production scheduler by 724% on SLA-safe goodput per dollar. Our predictive world model forecasts power constraints, simulates candidate scheduling decisions, and ranks them by economic outcome before execution. In simulated replay of ~1.5M requests of public production traces, we saw +724% average SLA-safe goodput per dollar and -84% GPU-hours versus the production scheduler. May I send you the one-page technical memo? May I send you the one-page technical memo? — Beckett

stage_b.honesty: Winner = BEST-SUPPORTED AMONG TESTED, not 'best possible'. Live ranking is the qualitative persona ensemble (LLM role-play under competing inbox-reality hypotheses; outcomes are counted choices) — a model-based judgment, UNCALIBRATED against real outreach outcomes. The funnel is a structural prior; its absolute levels are claims. Arms in within_noise_of_winner are indistinguishable at this draw count, and a hypothesis-fragile winner is flagged, not trusted. 1 generated drafts were rejected by the contract/fact guard.
